name: Los Martells & Cardo Boix - Circular route from Rasquera
name_original: Los Martells & Cardó Boix - Vuelta circular desde Rasquera
region_intro: Jogging around Rasquera offers diverse terrain, from the flat, scenic paths of the Val de Zafán Greenway to more challenging elevated routes. The region is characterized by its varied landscapes, including riverside vegetation, wooded areas, and mountainous terrain with significant elevation gains. Runners can explore deep ravines, isolated mountain ranges, and paths leading to panoramic viewpoints. Best jogging routes around Rasquera The most popular jogging route is Los Martells & Cardo Boix - Circular route from Rasquera, a 12.3 miles (19.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 53 minutes to complete. This difficult route features an elevation gain of over 850 meters. Another top favourite among local runners is Miravet Castle – Miravet Castle loop from Castell de Miravet, a difficult 11.8 miles (19.0 km) path. This route offers substantial climbing, with over 700 meters of elevation gain. Local runners also love the Miravet — Village and Riverside – Miravet Castle loop from Tarragona, a 7.5 miles (12.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 33 minutes. Jogging around Rasquera is defined by its mix of river plains, wooded areas, and elevated paths offering panoramic views. The network of running routes provides options for various fitness levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Rasquera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from a few reviews. More than 400 runners have used komoot to explore Rasquera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Rasquera?
A: There are over 45 dedicated running routes around Rasquera listed on komoot, offering a wide range of options for different fitness levels and preferences. This includes 1 easy route, 26 moderate routes, and 20 difficult routes.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running routes in Rasquera?
A: While most routes in Rasquera are moderate to difficult, the region does offer some easier sections. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10421743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Tarragona&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route with minimal elevation gain. Additionally, the Val de Zafán Greenway, a former railway line, provides extensive flat and comfortable sections, particularly in the Ebro Delta, which are ideal for a relaxed jog.

Q: What kind of challenging running routes can I find near Rasquera?
A: Rasquera is known for its more strenuous running options, with 20 routes classified as difficult. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e949547743/los-martells-cardo-boix-circular-route-from-rasquera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Martells & Cardo Boix - Circular route from Rasquera&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 19.8 km trail with over 850 meters of elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38156830" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miravet Castle – Miravet Castle loop from Castell de Miravet&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 19.0 km with over 700 meters of ascent.

Q: Can I combine my run with sightseeing or visiting landmarks in the Rasquera area?
A: Absolutely! Many running routes in Rasquera offer access to stunning natural and historical landmarks. You can find routes that pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/692380" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Creu de Santos Summit&lt;/a&gt;, offering magnificent views, or the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3984401" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Foradada Arch&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3413673" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lluminosa Cave&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5036000" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Sant Roc and Ronya Spring&lt;/a&gt;, providing interesting points of interest along your run.

Q: Are there running routes with panoramic views in Rasquera?
A: Yes, the mountainous terrain around Rasquera provides numerous opportunities for runs with breathtaking panoramic views. Routes that ascend to summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5312662" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Pins Summit&lt;/a&gt; or offer views from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5554769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tossal de Montagut&lt;/a&gt; are excellent choices for runners seeking scenic vistas. The region's elevated paths often provide expansive outlooks over deep ravines and isolated mountain ranges.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available in Rasquera?
A: Yes, Rasquera offers several circular running routes, which are great for starting and ending your run in the same location. A notable example is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e949547743/los-martells-cardo-boix-circular-route-from-rasquera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Martells & Cardo Boix - Circular route from Rasquera&lt;/a&gt;. Another option is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40344633" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bassa del Morral loop from Mas de Nacres&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Rasquera?
A: The running routes in Rasquera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Runners often praise the diverse terrain, which includes a mix of river plains, wooded areas, and elevated paths. The opportunity to explore picturesque natural scenery and enjoy panoramic views is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for families with children in Rasquera?
A: For families, the flatter sections of the Val de Zafán Greenway are an excellent choice. This former railway line is well-maintained and offers a safe, multi-use path suitable for various activities, including jogging with children. Its varied scenery, including riverside vegetation and poplars, makes for a pleasant family outing.

Q: What kind of natural scenery can I expect while running in Rasquera?
A: Jogging in Rasquera allows you to immerse yourself in diverse natural scenery. You'll encounter picturesque wooded areas, deep ravines, and isolated mountain ranges. The Val de Zafán Greenway, for instance, features pleasant corridors of poplars and riverside vegetation alongside rice plantations, offering a refreshing environment for your run.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by water features or springs?
A: While specific routes passing directly by waterfalls are not highlighted, the region does boast natural water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5036000" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Sant Roc and Ronya Spring&lt;/a&gt; is a point of interest that can be incorporated into a run. Additionally, the famous La Fontcalda, a warm spring, is a natural attraction in the area that could serve as a destination for a longer, exploratory run.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Rasquera?
A: Rasquera's climate makes it suitable for running for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for outdoor activities. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late evening runs are advisable. Winters are typically mild, making it possible to run year-round, though some higher elevation paths might be cooler.

Q: Are there any running routes that lead to historical sites like castles?
A: Yes, you can find routes that lead to or pass by historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31496723" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miravet — Village and Riverside – Miravet Castle loop from Tarragona&lt;/a&gt; is a 12.1 km trail that offers views of Miravet Castle, a significant historical landmark overlooking the Ebro river. These routes provide a unique blend of physical activity and cultural exploration.

Curious rock formation reminiscent of Thor's Hammer or maybe it's just a cinephile influence.

The path that goes up from the path of Sant Roc in the direction of Portell de Cardó, was quite lost, much better the one that continues until it connects again with this one.

Spectacular balma that pierces the ridge and was once arranged as a key access point to the valley. The dry stone zigzags the ridge to comfortably access from one side of the ridge to the other.
Known since ancient times as Lluminosa to differentiate it from the relatively nearby cave which, being a closed gallery, is known as Llòbrega.
Cited by Faura in his Collection (1909) and by Ferraté in Speleologia de les comarques Tarragona (1918), although neither gives more information than the name.
We are not aware of any specific cataloging work, although it is well known by hikers.
When the Mestral winds blow, which is quite common, the force of the wind increases dramatically as it passes through the Cave.
The trail offers stunning views of the Puertos de Beceite and Montsant mountains. It's an ideal hike for a sunny or windy day and is known for its scenic beauty and historical significance.
